jobs.name: Memory Dreaming Promotion
jobs.description: [managed-by=memory-core.short-term-promotion] Promote weighted short-term recalls into MEMORY.md (limit=10, minScore=0.800, minRecallCount=3, minUniqueQueries=3, recencyHalfLifeDays=14, maxAgeDays=30).

payload.message:
Backup workspace to GitHub. Run these commands:
1. cd ~/workspace
2. Run ~/workspace/bin/topic-sync to regenerate topic markdown from JSON
3. Export cron jobs: openclaw cron list --json > backups/cron-jobs.json 2>/dev/null || true
4. Copy config: cp ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json backups/openclaw.json
5. git add -A && git diff --cached --stat
6. If there are changes: git commit -m "Daily backup $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" && ~/workspace/bin/safe-push --yes
7. If no changes, stay silent.
Only report if a push failed.

DAILY SELF-EVAL. Follow ~/workspace/memory/self-eval-framework.md.

1. Read memory/mistakes.md and memory/current-weaknesses.md.
2. Use LCM tools (lcm_grep, lcm_expand_query) to review today's conversations. Look for: Andy corrections, repeated mistakes, wrong info given, work done instead of delegating, broken commitments, slow learning.
3. Also check git log --since=24h for commit patterns.
4. If new mistakes found: append to memory/mistakes.md (date | #category | one-line, then 2-3 lines max).
5. Review current-weaknesses.md — max 3 items, trigger→response format. Prune items not improving in 7 days.
6. Archive mistakes older than 30 days to mistakes-archive.md.
7. Git commit if changed.
8. Stay silent — weekly report handles Andy notification.

Run ClawVault observation on all active sessions. Execute:

1. `cd ~/workspace && CLAWVAULT_PATH=/home/clawd/workspace/memory clawvault observe --active --cron`
2. If observations were written, run `cd ~/workspace && git add -A && git commit -m "clawvault: nightly observations $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" && ~/workspace/bin/safe-push --yes`
3. Report number of sessions observed and observations written. If nothing new, stay silent.

Morning briefing for Andy. Four parts.

PART 1: HEARTBEAT
1. Run timeout 15s openclaw status. If it times out or fails, say so briefly.
2. Check for errors in logs from the last hour only.
3. Run timeout 20s env CLAWVAULT_PATH=/home/clawd/workspace/memory clawvault checkpoint. If it fails, note it and continue.
4. Check ~/workspace/memory/commitments/ for pending items only.
5. Always include a one-line heartbeat summary.

PART 2: CALENDAR
Run source ~/.openclaw/.env && timeout 20s curl -fsS -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-Api-Token: $N8N_WEBHOOK_TOKEN" -d '{"service":"calendar","action":"today"}' https://n8n-andy.xyz/webhook/openclaw-google
If it returns events, list them with AST times. If it fails or is empty, say calendar unavailable or no events.

PART 3: TASKS
Scan only the first 10 open or in-progress TaskNotes under /home/clawd/vaults/TaskNotes/Tasks/. List title, priority, and due date. Highlight overdue items. If none, say no open tasks.

PART 4: BRIEFING
1. Run timeout 10s curl -fsS wttr.in/Sint+Maarten?format=3
2. Run timeout 15s env CLAWVAULT_PATH=/home/clawd/workspace/memory clawvault search commitments

Return one concise morning update: system health, calendar, tasks, weather, commitments. Keep it short.

Memory hygiene review. Keep this bounded.
1. Run `env CLAWVAULT_PATH=/home/clawd/workspace/memory clawvault search "*"` once to list entries.
2. Review only up to 25 candidate entries, prioritizing commitments, decisions, and facts older than 30 days.
3. Delete only clearly obsolete items with `clawvault forget`. Do not do broad cleanup.
4. Summarize what was removed and what was kept.
5. If nothing needs cleanup, say so briefly and stop.

Weekly setup review. Check the environment and report only actionable issues. Keep it bounded.
1. Run `timeout 15s openclaw status`.
2. Review cron jobs and flag only disabled, failing, or stale ones.
3. Check disk, memory, and uptime.
4. Run `timeout 15s qmd status`; only mention it if pending embeddings are over 100 or the command fails.
5. Run `cd ~/workspace && git status --short`; flag only real drift.
6. Review `~/workspace/memory/commitments/` and `current-weaknesses.md` for stale items.
7. Check whether OpenClaw itself is outdated.
8. Skip deep stack-manifest diffing unless a clear stack change is already visible from the current environment.

Return a concise report. If everything is clean, say so in one line.

Check for a ClawVault update with bounded commands.
1. Run `timeout 20s npm view clawvault version` to get the latest version.
2. Run `timeout 10s clawvault --version` to get the installed version.
3. If the registry call fails, say registry unavailable and stop.
4. If versions differ, report the installed and latest versions.
5. If they match, stay silent.

Run ClawVault reflection to promote stable observations into weekly reflections. Execute:

1. `cd ~/workspace && CLAWVAULT_PATH=/home/clawd/workspace/memory clawvault reflect`
2. If reflections were promoted, also run `clawvault reweave` to mark superseded observations
3. Run `qmd update && qmd embed` to reindex
4. `cd ~/workspace && git add -A && git commit -m "clawvault: weekly reflection $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" && ~/workspace/bin/safe-push --yes`
5. Summarize what was reflected and reweaved. If nothing promoted, say so briefly.

WEEKLY SELF-EVAL REPORT. Follow ~/workspace/memory/self-eval-framework.md. Keep it concise and bounded.
1. Read this week files in `memory/self-evals/` and `memory/mistakes.md`.
2. Write `memory/self-evals/week-YYYY-WW.md` with what improved, what did not, and one concrete change for next week.
3. Commit only if the file changed.
4. Send Andy a concise Telegram summary under 10 lines with no fluff.
5. Hard stop after the summary. Do not do extra review, extra git archaeology, or side quests.

WEEKLY UPDATE CHECK: Run `npm outdated -g 2>/dev/null`, `openclaw status | grep Update`, and check lossless-claw version vs `npm show @martian-engineering/lossless-claw version`. Summarize what's outdated and notify Andy via Telegram (-1003709913328 topic 1) with a concise update report. Only notify if there are actual updates available. Include severity (major/minor/patch) for each.
